ab: kazu kana 2022 ’ami, pakaygi na séhu kana karuwa pusavak za tu ngadan na ’atayan i mibunsiyo na Yécumin.
ch: 在2022年，政府召開了原住民身分證可單列族名的會議。
en: In 2022, the government announced that indigenous identification cards can include tribal names separately.

ab: kiumaumalr ku lra kani Avarang mawna vangesaran i zekalr na sawariyan kiyami pakirev kana séhu za “pusavakaw za tu ngadan na ’atayan.” i mibunsiyo.
ch: 我訪問了長期向政府爭取身分證「單列族名」的部落青年Avarang。
en: I interviewed Avarang, a young tribal member who has long been advocating for the inclusion of tribal names on identification cards.

ab: kanini na kasede’an i inku zi i intaw makakuret mi kana auwa kanantu ruma’ kiumaumalr kantaw.
ch: 我和他約定好這個假日到他家訪問他。
en: We had arranged to visit his home this holiday for the interview.

ab: kazu kana kiumaumalran mu, pakalrazam kaninku zatu pinarevuwa’ tu ngadan, zi kirekalrazam ku tu niruma’enan, zi nantu zekalr.
ch: 在訪問的過程中，他向我介紹了他的名字的由來，也帶我認識了他的家人、他的部落。
en: During the interview, he introduced to me the origin of his name and also introduced me to his family and tribe.

ab: kiumalr ku kantaw kemuda zi marukerang patrutru kana puwavalis za ngadan, a awatr ziya na ipuvalray ’azi ’inava ziya maw?
ch: 我問他為什麼堅持要改名，舊的登記方式不好嗎?
en: I asked him why he insisted on changing his name. Wasn’t the old registration method sufficient?

ab: marengay kaninku i intaw, ’alrana pimanayaw za valray na minkuk ’azi karuwa patulre’ay paredek za tu ngadan, nu venavina a trau kantaw mu “izu i... eman” kema, zi mawna kemazu mupana lra a kalikeliked na sime’an na ngai.
ch: 他對我說，因為用中文字並不能準確的傳達他的名字，其他人還會叫「izu i... eman」，也因此鬧出不少笑話。
en: He told me that using Chinese characters could not accurately convey his name, and others would still call him \"izu i... eman,\" which resulted in many jokes being made.

ab: marengay i Avarang, “na vinavalray kanantu valray na Yéncumin na ngadan mu maw tatena’ nanta ngadan. nanku ngadan paruveli kananta ’inalrayan, nanta niruma’enan, nanta kinayanan, kiyalriman kana kazuwan ta mawan atrevung tu ngadan i intaw.”
ch: Avarang說：「原住民語書寫的名字才是我們真正的名字。我的名字代表了我們的族群、我們的家族、我們的身分，希望能有更多族人找回自己的名字。」
en: Avarang said, \"The names written in indigenous languages are our true names. My name represents our ethnic group, our family, our identity. I hope more fellow tribespeople can reclaim their own names.\"
